title: Values should not be uselessly incremented
description: Values should not be uselessly incrementedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugunusedWhy is this an issue?A value that is incremented or decremented and then not stored is at best wasted code and at worst a bug.Noncompliant code examplefunction pickNumber() { let i = 0; i = i++; // Noncompliant; i is still zero return i++; // Noncompliant; 0 returned }Compliant solutionfunction pickNumber() { let i = 0; i++; return ++i; }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Useless post-increment: the variable is assigned its value before incrementing (e.g., i = i++). This has no effect.
Useless post-decrement: the variable is assigned its value before decrementing (e.g., i = i--). This has no effect.
Useless post-increment in return: the old value is returned, then the variable is incremented. Consider using pre-increment (++i) instead.
Useless post-decrement in return: the old value is returned, then the variable is decremented. Consider using pre-decrement (--i) instead.
Useless post-increment in expression: the variable is assigned its value before incrementing. This has no effect.
Useless post-decrement in expression: the variable is assigned its value before decrementing. This has no effect.
